exchanges.user:
We want to go over all the rust crates (both under rust_pkg and under crack_demo) and for each crate we run 4 coder sub-agents to make it do what we want. Then, we use the wait join command to wait for all the sub-agent to finish, and we take notes from their reports and move on to the next 4 sub-agent for the next 4 crates until we are done with all crates.

Here's what we want them to do : we want them to enable the warning for missing module docstring, and for missing docstring on public function or structure or enum, and then to go over all the rust modules inside src/ for that respective crate and to add a module docstring at the top of the module, and to add dosctring for every public function and struct. This module docstring will be a couple of paragraphs long and explain with proper links what the module is doing , why it is doing that, who is using it, who it uses to do the thing it must do, focus on both the why and the how and the when and the where - but keep it short. The public function/struct docstrings will be much shorter, usually, and will contain general information about what the function does, what its invariants are, what it returns, who would want to use it, any gotchas, etc. Do NOT edit any existing docstrings or module docstrings! We ONLY want to write missing docstrings, never edit any existing ones !  

Before you start working on the crates, figure out how to make all the crates inherit the warning configuration from the workspace, and set up only in the workspace the warning we want. Then, edit yourself the Cargo.toml for all the sub-workspaces, so we get the warnings on build. Finally, go ahead with the actual work.

The coder sub-agents should figure out on their own if they want to run a sub-agent - if they do (for example in the bevy game demo_resolution_etc) it would be valuable to have a sub-agent for each main game plugin. Do your own quick analysis of the code size and tell the sub-agents if they are allowed or not to use sub-agents of their own (prefferably no, for most crates, yes for the bevy game).

The subagents as well as yourself are only allowed to write and edit docstrings in rust files (under rust_pkg and crack_demo) . Only you should also edit the Cargo.toml files as stated above. The sub-agents should only have a maximum of one (1) sub-agent of their own, which they will wait for to be done using the tool. So do not spawn too many sub-agents in parallel - and always wait for them to finish. Use "cargo build" on each crate to view the warnings and tell each sub-agent to fix only the warnings related to missing docstrings - no other type of warning is to be fixed (as that would mean fixing rust code).

turns.thinking:
Let me break down this task:

1. First, I need to understand the project structure - find all Rust crates under `rust_pkg` and `crack_demo`
2. Set up workspace-level warning configuration for missing docstrings
3. Update Cargo.toml files to inherit these warnings
4. Run cargo build to see warnings
5. Spawn coder sub-agents (4 at a time) to add missing docstrings
6. Wait for them to finish, then move to next batch

Let me start by exploring the project structure.
Let me look at the workspace Cargo.toml first to understand the structure, then examine each crate.

tool_blocks.name: read
tool_blocks.output:
[package]
name = "crack"

[package.edition]
workspace = true

[package.version]
workspace = true

[package.authors]
workspace = true

[features]
default = []
web_serviceworker_worker = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackslave"]
web_serviceworker_loader = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackloader"]
native_thread_worker = ["dep:thread_crackworker"]


[dependencies.consensus_crackhead]
path = "./rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead"

[dependencies.net_crackpipe]
path = "./rust_pkg/net_crackpipe"

[dependencies.storage_crackhouse]
path = "./rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse"

[dependencies.api_asscrack]
path = "./rust_pkg/api_asscrack"

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackloader]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader"
optional = true

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackslave]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave"
optional = true

[dependencies.thread_crackworker]
path = "./rust_pkg/thread_crackworker"
optional = true

[workspace]
members = [
    "rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead",
    "rust_pkg/net_crackpipe",
    "rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave",

    "rust_pkg/api_asscrack",
    "rust_pkg/thread_crackworker",
    "rust_pkg/_crack_utils",
    
    "crack_demo/web_worker",
    "crack_demo/thread_worker",
    "crack_demo/game_logic",
    "crack_demo/demo_resolution_selector_web_bevy",
    "crack_demo/chat_cli",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
authors = ["Your Name <you@example.com>"]
edition = "2024"

[workspace.dependencies]
anyhow = "1"
wasm-bindgen-test = "0.3"
js-sys = "0.3.98"
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "1"
tracing = "0.1"
wasm-bindgen = "=0.2.121"
wasm-bindgen-futures = "=0.4.71"
web-sys = "0.3.98"

[workspace.dependencies.serde]
version = "1"
features = ["derive"]

[workspace.dependencies.tokio]
version = "1"
features = ["full"]

[workspace.lints.clippy]
new_without_default = "allow"
unused-async = "warn"

[profile.android-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0

[profile.dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev.build-override]
opt-level = 3
codegen-units = 16
debug = 0

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = "fat"
codegen-units = 1
debug = 0
strip = true

[profile.server-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0
